title: Magnetic Flux Leakage (MFL) — pipeline & rail inspection
sub_domain: Ferromagnetic defect detection via DC flux
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical NDT/NDE literature for this principle.

E.description: Magnetic Flux Leakage (MFL) — pipeline & rail inspection: magnetic flux leakage produces the measurement through a 4-node primitive DAG L.dc_magnetize -> L.flux_leakage -> D.hall_sensor -> int.spatial, with spatially-projected accumulation and additive Gaussian thermal/electronic noise. Recovery is posed as a non-convex inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 2D defect geometry. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~10; calibration-level mismatch (liftoff_variation, magnetization_non_uniformity, external_interference) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: B_leak(x,y) = integral m(defect(r)) * kernel(r - (x,y), lift_off, velocity) + n
E.world_state_x: Defect / strain / composition map depending on principle

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 2D defect geometry is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness is local rather than global (non-convex landscape); convergence depends on initialisation and priors. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 10); liftoff_variation dominates the stability cliff; magnetization_non_uniformity and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Additive gaussian thermal/electronic noise sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while mild Tikhonov or analytic inversion is sufficient at the nominal Omega point.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [Dipole-Model] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [NN-MFL, Deep-MFL]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(N_measurements). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |liftoff_variation| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
